prevalence.source: WHO scabies burden estimates; tropical dermatology surveillance
prevalence.trend: stable; persistent outbreaks in crowded communities

incidence.population: General population; higher in schools and crowded households
incidence.source: Community prevalence studies and outbreak investigations

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high itch burden and secondary infection risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical pruritic papules and burrows; diagnosis usually clinical; dermoscopy/microscopy for confirmation when available

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of cases annually in endemic settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; requires treatment of close contacts and hygiene measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care and dermatology clinics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical permethrin or oral ivermectin; treat household contacts; wash bedding/clothing
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Reinfestation due to crowded living conditions and incomplete contact treatment
